Regression Pipeline  /  In-Browser  /  Zero Install

Regression
Pipeline

Upload any labeled CSV, engineer features with per-column scaling, then train and compare multiple regression models — including Facebook Prophet — entirely in your browser.

Linear / Ridge / Lasso K-NN · Decision Tree Random Forest · GBM 🔮 Facebook Prophet R² · RMSE · MAE · MAPE
8
Models
4
Metrics
0
Dependencies

📥 Load Dataset

Upload any CSV with a numeric target column. After loading, select the target and classify features.

Select Pipeline Mode
📐
Regression
Predict a numeric target from features using multiple ML models.
✓
📈
Forecast
Predict future values from a time series using Prophet trend + seasonality.
📂

Drop a CSV file here or click to browse

Any CSV with a header row and a continuous numeric target column.
Supports numeric and low-cardinality categorical features.

Step 1 of 2 — Select Target Column

Click the column you want to predict. Must be numeric with multiple distinct values.

Step 2 of 2 — Classify Feature Columns

Set data type for each feature. Override auto-detection if needed. Uncheck to exclude a column.

Supported formats: Standard CSV with a header row. Target column must be numeric (e.g. price, temperature, sales). For Prophet, include a date/timestamp column — assign it in Step 4.

🔍 Explore Data

Preview rows, inspect statistics, and understand target distribution before modeling.

📭

Load data in Step 1 first

⚙️ Feature Preprocessing

Choose a scaling or transformation for each numeric feature. Defaults are suggested based on each column's distribution.

Tip: Distance-based models (K-NN, Linear Regression) benefit from StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler. Tree models are scale-invariant. Skewed distributions may benefit from Log1p. Prophet handles raw values internally.
📭

Load data in Step 1 first

⚗ Experiment Variants
Snapshot preprocessing configs and compare how feature engineering choices affect regression error across all selected models.
Forecast Horizon
7 365
PERIODS AHEAD 30
Confidence Interval

🔮 Prophet Configuration

Fourier series captures weekly & yearly seasonality.

Fourier Order

🎛️ Configure Models

Select regressors to train, adjust the train/test split, and configure Prophet if selected.

Select Regression Models
📐
Linear Reg.
SGD · L2=0 · OLS
✓
🔒
Ridge
L2 regularization
✓
✂️
Lasso
L1 · sparse features
✓
🔵
K-NN Regressor
Neighborhood mean · k=7
✓
🌲
Decision Tree
CART · MSE · depth 8
✓
🌳
Random Forest
20 trees · bagging
✓
⚡
Grad. Boosting
Sequential trees · MSE
✓
🔮
FB Prophet
Trend + Fourier seas.
✓

🔮 Prophet Configuration

Prophet decomposes data into trend + weekly & yearly seasonality via Fourier series. Assign a date column to unlock temporal features — without it, row index is used as the time axis.

Date / Time Column (optional)
Fourier Order
Train / Test Split
TRAIN TEST
TRAIN 80%  //  TEST 20%  //  SEED 42
No experiment variants saved. Go to Step 3 to save preprocessing variants for comparison.

⚡ Training

Models are trained in-browser using pure JavaScript — no server or Python required.

⏳

Awaiting pipeline run

📊 Results

Regression evaluation — R², RMSE, MAE, MAPE, scatter plots, residuals, feature importance.

📭

Train models first

🔮 Predict on New Data

Upload an unlabelled CSV — all trained models generate predictions side-by-side.

⚠ Notice: Train at least one model in Step 5 before generating predictions.
Upload Prediction CSV
📂

Drop unlabelled CSV here or click to browse

Must contain the same feature columns as your training set. Target column is not required.

Built with vanilla JS  //  Chart.js  //  PapaParse  //  Runs entirely in the browser  //  Facebook Prophet (JS approximation)
